canonical_definition: An attack procedure that inserts copied original text into generated text to test watermark robustness.
papers: A Semantic Invariant Robust Watermark for Large Language Models
Towards Codable Watermarking for Injecting Multi-Bits Information to LLMs

definitions.text: An attack procedure that inserts copied original text into generated text to test watermark robustness.
definitions.paper: A Semantic Invariant Robust Watermark for Large Language Models

definitions.text: An evaluation procedure to test watermark robustness where a segment of watermarked text is inserted into a larger body of human-written text.
definitions.paper: Towards Codable Watermarking for Injecting Multi-Bits Information to LLMs

relationships.evidence: In Table 1, we list the detection accuracy of our watermark algorithm and various baseline algorithms under the no-attack setting and when texts are rewritten using GPT3.5, two different DIPPER settings (Krishna et al., 2023) and the copy-paste attack Kirchenbauer et al. (2023b). (Section 6.2)
